metadata.title: BurstPick for Mac - AI Burst Photo Culling for Lightroom Workflows
metadata.description: Cull thousands of burst photos on your Mac with on-device AI. Group sequences, rank keepers, export XMP ratings and color labels, and hand off to Lightroom Classic with experimental Pro catalog export.

hero.tagline: AI burst photo culling for Mac photographers.
hero.description: Cull sports, wildlife, and event bursts locally on your Mac. BurstPick groups near-identical sequences, scores sharpness, exposure, noise, faces, and aesthetics, then exports XMP ratings and color labels for Lightroom workflows. Free starts with up to 50,000 photos per catalog.

showcase.heading: Built by Photographers, for Photographers
showcase.description: We know the burst workflow because we live it. Wildlife action sequences, indoor sports at 30fps, event coverage with thousands of frames. We built BurstPick because we were tired of the grind.

story.heading: From Thousands to Your Top Picks
story.description: Every shoot produces hundreds of near-identical frames. BurstPick groups them, scores them, and puts the best ones first. Seconds instead of hours. You pick the winners.
story.before_desc: Hundreds of near-identical burst shots filling your screen. Where do you even start?
story.after_desc: Your top picks ranked by sharpness, exposure, face quality, and aesthetics, ready for review

features.heading: Cull Burst Photos Faster on Mac
features.subheading: Go from thousands of burst shots to your best picks. Photo analysis stays on your device.
features.clustering_desc: No more scrolling through 200 near-identical frames. BurstPick recognizes each burst sequence and groups it for you, so you start with organized sets instead of a wall of thumbnails.
features.scoring_title: AI Photo Culling Scores You Can Override
features.scoring_desc: Every photo is scored on sharpness, exposure, noise, composition, and face quality. The strongest candidates rise to the top. You just confirm or override.
features.raw_desc: 33 RAW formats, 11 standard image formats, and video files for shoot context. ARW, CR2, CR3, NEF, DNG, HEIC, AVIF, WebP, JPEG XL, and more.
features.face_title: Find Every Face in the Shoot
features.face_desc: Detects faces and groups them by person across your entire session. Great for events: find every shot of a specific subject in seconds.
features.compare_desc: Put up to 4 finalists next to each other with synced zoom and color-accurate previews. See the difference down to the pixel.
features.workflow_desc: Keep, reject, star-rate, color-label. Every decision autosaves. Your originals are never touched.
features.lightroom_desc: Export ratings and color labels as XMP sidecars, with an experimental Pro Lightroom Classic catalog handoff when you need it. Photo Mechanic, BurstPick, Lightroom.
features.c2pa_desc: Culling and scoring run locally. Purchases, optional model downloads, updates, and opt-in telemetry can use network services, but your photos are not uploaded for analysis.

useCases.heading: Built for High-Volume Shoots
useCases.subheading: BurstPick is tuned for sessions where the hard part is choosing between hundreds of near-identical keepers.

sports.title: Sports Photography
sports.body: Review 30fps sequences, compare near-misses, and keep the sharpest frame from each play.

wildlife.title: Wildlife Photography
wildlife.body: Group long action bursts and surface frames with clean focus, pose, and composition.

events.title: Event Photography
events.body: Find faces across a session, rate selects, and export XMP metadata for editing.

gallery.heading: See BurstPick in Action
gallery.subheading: Built for speed and accuracy. Here's what the interface looks like.
gallery.main_ui_desc: Clusters on the left, your selected photo front and center, filmstrip at the bottom. Everything one glance away.
gallery.all_photos_desc: Browse every imported photo with filters, smart collections, labels, picks, rejects, and rating controls in one dense view.
gallery.clustering_desc: Each burst sequence grouped automatically. Scroll through sets, not hundreds of loose frames.
gallery.scoring_desc: Every photo ranked by sharpness, exposure, noise, face quality, and aesthetics. Adjust the weights to match how you shoot.
gallery.metadata_desc: Check camera, lens, exposure, dimensions, file size, and white balance details without leaving the culling workspace.
gallery.compare_desc: Put your top picks side by side with synced zoom. Spot the winner instantly.
gallery.survey_desc: Review similar shots together, then pick, reject, rate, or color-label candidates directly from the comparison strip.
gallery.pro_iap_desc: The in-app purchase flow clearly shows the free tier, Pro limits, Lightroom handoff, and local-first workflow.
gallery.persons_desc: Every person in your shoot, grouped automatically. Jump straight to the subject you need.
gallery.scoring_food_desc: Drill into AI scores for any photo: sharpness, exposure, noise, face quality, and aesthetics, all broken down at a glance.
gallery.scoring_whiskey_desc: See how BurstPick handles challenging low-light shots and finds the best candidate.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: 33 RAW formats (ARW, CR2, CR3, NEF, DNG, ORF, RW2, RAF, and more), 11 standard image formats, and 15 video formats for shoot context.
faq.a2: BurstPick is free to start. Pro is a $89.99 lifetime in-app purchase through the Mac App Store. No subscription, no recurring fees.
faq.a3: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later, Apple Silicon or Intel. Apple Silicon is recommended for the fastest CoreML and Neural Engine performance. Windows remains on the roadmap.
faq.q4: Does it require an internet connection?
faq.a4: No internet is required for culling or scoring. StoreKit purchases, optional model downloads, update checks, and opt-in telemetry can use the network, but photos are not uploaded for analysis.
faq.q5: How many ML models does it use?
faq.a5: BurstPick supports 20+ on-device model providers across image quality, aesthetics, similarity embeddings, face recognition, VLM assessment, and image classification. Basic no-download scoring is available on Free; downloadable model families require Pro.
faq.a6: No. BurstPick is non-destructive by default. Ratings, color labels, and review state are saved as catalog or sidecar metadata; originals are only moved when you explicitly choose a move workflow.
faq.a7: Yes. Ratings and color labels export as standard XMP sidecars. The Pro Lightroom Classic catalog handoff is experimental; pick/reject flags do not have a universal sidecar standard, so BurstPick uses catalog and best-effort metadata handling where possible.
faq.a8: A team of photographers and macOS engineers who shoot 10,000+ frames per session. We built it because we needed it. Existing tools didn't get burst workflows.
faq.q9: How does BurstPick fit into my workflow?
faq.a9: It's a companion, not a replacement. Ingest with Photo Mechanic, cull with BurstPick, then edit in Lightroom or your existing editor. Export ratings and color labels as XMP sidecars; experimental Lightroom Classic catalog handoff is available in Pro.
faq.a10: Use the Mac App Store link on this site to open the current listing. The Mac version is the launch platform; Windows remains on the roadmap.
faq.q11: Is BurstPick a Mac App Store app?
faq.a11: Yes. BurstPick launches on macOS through the Mac App Store. The current supported platform is Mac; Windows remains on the roadmap.
faq.q12: Can I use BurstPick before buying Pro?
faq.a12: Yes. Free supports up to 50,000 photos per catalog, basic no-download scoring, burst grouping, non-destructive review, and unlimited XMP sidecar export.
faq.q13: Does BurstPick upload my RAW files?
faq.a13: No. Culling and scoring run locally on your Mac. Network access is used only for StoreKit purchases, optional model downloads, update checks, and opt-in telemetry.
faq.a14: No. BurstPick is for culling before editing. Export XMP ratings and color labels, then edit in Lightroom or your existing workflow.

cta.heading: Ready to find your best shots?
cta.description: We built BurstPick because we spent more time culling than shooting. Native macOS app, free to start, Pro lifetime purchase, no cloud upload for culling.
cta.notify_description: Get release notes, model updates, and workflow notes by email.
cta.requirements: macOS 14+ · Apple Silicon & Intel · Free tier available
cta.privacy: Photo analysis runs on-device. Your photos are not uploaded for culling.
cta.successMessage: You're subscribed. We'll send only BurstPick product updates.
cta.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again.
cta.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.

footer.tagline: Burst photo culling, built by photographers who shoot 10K+ frames a day.
footer.built_with: Native macOS app built with Swift, SwiftUI, Vision, CoreML, and Metal.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.intro: BurstPick is built for a local-first workflow. We designed the app so your photos and scoring data stay on your device. This policy explains what data BurstPick accesses, what it transmits, and what it does not.
privacyPage.localProcessingBody: Photo analysis, burst clustering, ML scoring, preference learning, and face grouping run on your device using Apple CoreML, Vision, and Metal where available. Your photos are never uploaded to any server for culling. Catalogs, XMP sidecars, and scoring data are stored locally in open formats (SQLite, XMP).
privacyPage.dataCollectionBody: BurstPick does not track you and does not collect photos, personal media metadata, advertising identifiers, or tracking identifiers. Local diagnostic events may be stored on your Mac to help troubleshoot the app; remote telemetry is opt-in and disabled by default.
privacyPage.networkBody: BurstPick uses network access only for bounded app services: App Store purchases and restore through Apple's StoreKit framework, optional model downloads, optional update checks, and opt-in telemetry if enabled by the user. BurstPick works offline for photo analysis and scoring; photos are not uploaded for culling.
privacyPage.permissionsBody: BurstPick requests file-system read access to import photos and write access to save XMP sidecars and catalogs. No camera or photo library access is required. All permissions are scoped to user-selected folders via macOS sandboxed file access.
privacyPage.retentionBody: Because BurstPick does not transmit your photos or personal data to our servers, there is no remote data retention by BurstPick. Apple may retain purchase transaction records under their own privacy policy.
privacyPage.thirdPartyBody: BurstPick does not include advertising or tracking SDKs. StoreKit purchase processing is handled by Apple under Apple's privacy policy. Downloadable model assets and update metadata are fetched from BurstPick-controlled release infrastructure when requested.
privacyPage.childrenBody: BurstPick does not collect any data from any user, including children. The app contains no user accounts, social features, or data collection mechanisms.
privacyPage.contactBody: If you have privacy questions, please reach out to our support team at support@burstpick.app.

termsPage.title: Terms of Service
termsPage.intro: By downloading or using BurstPick, you agree to these terms.
termsPage.licenseBody: BurstPick is provided by Xylolabs Inc. under a personal commercial license. Free features are available without a Pro purchase; Pro is unlocked through a lifetime in-app purchase on the Mac App Store. You may not share, transfer, or sublicense your license to another individual or organization without prior written consent.
termsPage.acceptableUseBody: You may use BurstPick on devices you own, subject to App Store and StoreKit entitlement rules. The license is personal and non-transferable — sharing accounts or credentials with others is not permitted. You are responsible for your own media, backups, and workflow.
termsPage.noWarrantyBody: BurstPick is provided "as is", without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including fitness for a particular purpose.
termsPage.liabilityBody: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Xylolabs Inc. is not liable for damages arising from use or inability to use the software.
termsPage.openSourceBody: BurstPick includes open-source dependencies that remain subject to their own licenses.
termsPage.changesBody: These terms may be updated over time. Continued use of BurstPick after updates means you accept the revised terms.
termsPage.generalBody: Any matters not explicitly covered by these terms shall be governed by applicable laws and generally accepted commercial practices.
termsPage.contactBody: For legal or licensing questions, please contact our support team.

pricing.heading: Simple, Honest Pricing
pricing.free_desc: Get started with burst culling. No cost, no catch.
pricing.pro_desc: For photographers who shoot thousands of frames per session.
pricing.pro_f5: AI Culling Assistant and contact sheets
pricing.pro_cta: Get on the Mac App Store
pricing.guarantee: No subscription. One-time in-app purchase, yours forever.

notDo.heading: What BurstPick Does NOT Do
notDo.subheading: BurstPick does one thing: helps you find your best shots faster.
notDo.item1_desc: BurstPick helps you decide which photos to keep. It's not a photo editor. Your RAW files stay untouched.
notDo.item2_desc: It works alongside your existing tools. Cull with BurstPick, edit in Lightroom. They fit together.
notDo.item3_desc: Culling and scoring run on your device. Network services do not receive your photos for analysis.

discord.title: Join Our Community
discord.description: Talk shop with other photographers, share your workflow, and help shape what BurstPick builds next.

about.heading: Built Native. Runs On-Device.
about.description: BurstPick is a native macOS app built in Swift and SwiftUI, tuned for Apple Silicon and still compatible with Intel Macs. Windows remains on the roadmap. Photo analysis stays on your machine.
about.native_desc: No web wrapper, no Electron. Native macOS performance with a UI that belongs on the platform.
about.ondevice_desc: Scoring models run locally through CoreML, Vision, and Metal. Your photos are scored and ranked without cloud upload.
about.silicon_desc: Uses Metal GPU acceleration and the Neural Engine on Apple Silicon. About 90 seconds for 10,000+ photos on M-series chips.
about.privacy_desc: No account is required for culling. Purchases, optional model downloads, and updates can use the network; photo analysis works offline.

downloadPage.title: Download BurstPick for Mac
downloadPage.description: Download BurstPick from the Mac App Store. Cull burst photos locally on macOS, export XMP ratings and color labels, and start free with up to 50,000 photos per catalog.
downloadPage.version: Available through the Mac App Store
downloadPage.notify_prompt: Subscribe for release notes, model updates, and workflow notes.
downloadPage.req_chip_value: Apple Silicon or Intel (Apple Silicon recommended)
downloadPage.req_disk_value: ~500 MB app install, plus optional downloadable ML models
downloadPage.privacy_note: All processing happens on your device. No photos are sent anywhere.
downloadPage.windows_description: The App Store launch is macOS-first. Windows remains on the roadmap, but the current supported app is for Mac.

auth.verifyEmailDesc: We've sent a verification link to your email address. Please check your inbox.
auth.deviceLoginDesc: Authorize BurstPick on your Mac to access your account.
auth.checkEmail: Check your email for a reset link.

modelsPage.title: ML Models
modelsPage.intro: BurstPick supports 20+ on-device model providers across 6 categories. Built-in models run without setup; larger model families download on demand and require Pro. Photos are not uploaded for analysis.

imageQuality.title: Image Quality Assessment
imageQuality.description: Scores each photo on sharpness, noise, exposure, and perceptual clarity. Used to rank photos within burst clusters.

aestheticScore.title: Aesthetic Scoring
aestheticScore.description: Rates artistic appeal based on composition, color harmony, and visual balance. Trained on large-scale human preference data.

imageEmbedding.title: Image Embedding
imageEmbedding.description: Turns photos into vectors for similarity clustering. Groups burst sequences and flags duplicates or near-duplicates.

faceEmbedding.title: Face Embedding
faceEmbedding.description: Builds face identity vectors for person grouping. Clusters photos by who appears in them.

vlmAssessment.title: Vision Language Model (VLM)
vlmAssessment.description: Reads photo content using natural language. Gives scene descriptions and quality reasoning that go beyond numerical scores.

imageClassification.title: Image Classification
imageClassification.description: Tags photos with scene and object labels for filtering and organization. Uses Apple's Vision framework.

modelsPage.footer: All models run as CoreML on your Mac using CPU, GPU, and Neural Engine where available. Download sizes are for the compressed CoreML package. Memory figures are approximate GPU/ANE usage during inference.

supportPage.title: Support
supportPage.intro: Need help with BurstPick? We're here to assist. Reach out through any of the channels below.
supportPage.emailBody: For technical issues, purchase questions, or feature requests, email us directly. We answer business-day support as capacity allows, with Pro requests prioritized.
supportPage.discordBody: Join our Discord server to chat with other photographers, share your workflow, get quick tips, and stay updated on new releases.
supportPage.faqBody: Check our FAQ section on the homepage for answers to common questions about supported formats, system requirements, pricing, Lightroom integration, and more.
supportPage.req2: Apple Silicon or Intel processor (Apple Silicon recommended)
supportPage.req3: ~500 MB app install, plus optional downloadable ML models
supportPage.responseBody: Email support is handled on business days, with Pro requests prioritized. Discord community questions often get faster responses from both the team and fellow photographers.
